metadata.title: Build an STM Tunneling Curve with Claude Code
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = tunneling curve. CRIMSON = 7x marker points. INK for all Text.

beats.narration_text: Medhavy A-I. From Sanskrit вҖ” meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude вҖ” plot tunneling current versus tip-to-sample distance for an S-T-M. Work function phi equals four electron-volts. Use the W-K-B formula: current proportional to e to the minus two kappa d. kappa equals root two m phi over h-bar. Show d from one to ten angstroms on a semi-log axis. Mark the point where d increases by one angstrom and the current drops by seven-point-four. Specify: the exponential decay, the slope, and the seven-times rule.

props.command:
claude "plot STM tunneling current vs gap d:
  ПҶ=4 eV, Оә=вҲҡ(2mПҶ)/в„Ҹ вүҲ 1.02 Г…вҒ»В№
  I вҲқ exp(вҲ’2Оәd)   [WKB tunneling]
  semi-log axis: d вҲҲ [1,10] Г…
  mark: +1 Г… вҶ’ I drops by factor eВІвүҲ7.4"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. Read before running. Two checks. First: the kappa value. The formula is root of two m phi over h-bar. For phi equals four electron-volts, kappa must come out near one-point-zero-two inverse angstroms. If it reads one-point-four-four, the code used the wrong unit conversion. Second: the slope. On a natural-log plot, the slope should equal minus two kappa вҖ” approximately minus two-point-zero-four per angstrom. Thirty seconds. Catch it before the render.

props.code:
# stm.py  вҖ”  Claude Code output
import numpy as np

# WKB tunneling: I вҲқ exp(вҲ’2Оәd)
hbar_eVs = 6.582e-16   # eVВ·s
m_eV    = 0.511e6      # eV/cВІ
phi_eV  = 4.0          # work function (eV)
c_Angps = 2.998e10     # Г…/s (speed of light)

kappa = np.sqrt(2 * m_eV * phi_eV) / (hbar_eVs * c_Angps)
# kappa вүҲ 1.024 Г…вҒ»В№  вҶ’  2Оә вүҲ 2.048 Г…вҒ»В№
# +1 Г… вҶ’ current Г— exp(вҲ’2.048) вүҲ 1/7.7

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two checks. First: the slope. On the semi-log plot the teal curve is a straight line. Its slope reads minus two kappa вҖ” approximately minus two-point-zero-four inverse angstroms. Every extra angstrom of gap multiplies the log-current by that slope. Second: the seven-point-four markers. The two crimson dots вҖ” one angstrom apart вҖ” show a current ratio of e-squared, approximately seven-point-four. That is Binnig and Rohrer's rule: pull the tip back by one angstrom and the current falls by a factor of seven. The periodic table of surface atoms is built on that sensitivity.

Iterate: claude вҖ” update stm dot py вҖ” add a second curve for phi equals five electron-volts. Steeper slope, faster drop. One diff, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, check, change. The tunneling curve was the vehicle. The loop is the takeaway.

props.command:
claude "update stm.py:
  вҶ’ add curve for ПҶ=5 eV (steeper slope)
  вҶ’ label both curves"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
